jokes.text: Any one at which I'm not serving hors d'oeuvres or pouring champagne.
jokes.setup: Asked about his idea of the perfect wedding
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry response about not wanting to work at weddings
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Geoffrey - his dry wit about being staff at fancy events. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: Wow, then you're gonna think ours sucks.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey saying he doesn't want to work weddings
jokes.punchline: Will realizing Geoffrey will have to work their wedding
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to previous line. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: My fault. For a second there, I forgot who I was talking to, you know that?
jokes.setup: Asking Uncle Phil not to be late for dinner
jokes.punchline: Will immediately realizing the irony of asking Phil to be punctual
jokes.explanation: Good character awareness joke - Will recognizing Phil's habits. Well-delivered self-correction.

jokes.text: Three hundred? Who are you inviting, the entire Wayans family?
jokes.setup: Phil announces 300 wedding guests as a 'small' wedding
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated comparison to the large Wayans comedy family
jokes.explanation: Smart pop culture reference that works on multiple levels - the Wayans are both numerous and in comedy. Good economy and delivery.

jokes.text: Oops. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will asking if he can invite his mom to his own wedding
jokes.punchline: Phil's awkward realization he forgot Will's mother on the guest list
jokes.explanation: Simple awkward moment. Phil's uncomfortable laugh sells it but it's fairly basic comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, what the hell was I thinking?
jokes.setup: Phil explaining the wedding is really for him, not Will
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic Southern accent reaction to Phil's selfishness
jokes.explanation: Will's accent work adds physical comedy element. Good character moment showing his frustration.

jokes.text: And I'm going to network like it's 1999.
jokes.setup: Phil explaining he's paying so it's his party
jokes.punchline: Phil's play on Prince's 'Party Like It's 1999' but making it about networking
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Phil's character - turning a party anthem into networking motivation. Great economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: No.
jokes.setup: Will asking if anyone in the house cares what he wants
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's brutally honest, immediate response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - his deadpan honesty delivered with maximum economy. The timing and character integration are flawless.

jokes.text: Like Carlton Co.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she has money in companies she's never heard of
jokes.punchline: Revealing Carlton created a fake company with his own name
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Carlton's schemes and Hilary's obliviousness. Sets up ongoing plot thread.

jokes.text: Smile, be polite...and wear a spandex dress that makes them sit up and say, 'Aye, chihuahua.'
jokes.setup: Carlton advising Hilary how to handle IRS audit
jokes.punchline: His suggestion to use sex appeal with the 'aye chihuahua' detail
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's misguided confidence and inappropriate advice. The specific phrase 'aye chihuahua' adds character color.

jokes.text: I'll wear leather. Why don't you just wear hot pants and a bustier with two big cones on it?
jokes.setup: Hilary agreeing to wear leather instead of spandex
jokes.punchline: Ashley's sarcastic suggestion escalating to Madonna-like costume
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with specific Madonna reference that fits the era. Ashley's sarcasm works well.

jokes.text: Before Memorial Day?
jokes.setup: Ashley suggesting revealing outfit
jokes.punchline: Hilary's concern is about fashion timing, not appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely missing the moral issue and focusing on fashion rules. Shows her priorities beautifully.

jokes.text: Those are flowers, Fred. You know, like a bride carries at a wedding.
jokes.setup: Fred asking about Phil's perennials in the garden
jokes.punchline: Will's pointed reminder about wedding context
jokes.explanation: Will trying to steer conversation toward wedding discussion. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Whoa, Fred.
jokes.setup: Fred escalating his demands for wedding in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Phil's overwhelmed reaction to Fred's momentum
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Phil being steamrolled by Fred's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I look like a call girl. A very expensive call girl.
jokes.setup: Hilary worrying about her revealing outfit
jokes.punchline: Her 'correction' that she looks expensive, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary - her vanity makes her focus on being 'expensive' rather than appropriate. Perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, that's ridiculous, it isn't that short.
jokes.setup: Carlton warning Hilary she could go to prison
jokes.punchline: Hilary thinking he's talking about her skirt length
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke. Hilary completely misunderstanding the context shows her self-absorption perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, this is a serious waste of a Wonderbra.
jokes.setup: Agent Dailey turns out to be a woman
jokes.punchline: Hilary's disappointment about her seduction outfit being useless
jokes.explanation: Great specific brand reference and perfect Hilary reaction. Shows her practical approach to using her looks.

jokes.text: Oh, she's focusing, all right, on your chest. I mean, as unbelievable and disgusting as it sounds, she wants you.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinking the female IRS agent isn't focusing
jokes.punchline: Carlton's horrified realization that she's attracted to Phil
jokes.explanation: Carlton's disgust at the idea of someone finding Phil attractive. Good character-specific reaction.

jokes.text: Hey, man, why you ain't tell me you invited the Ritzy's?
jokes.setup: Fred complaining about Phil's 'ritzy friends'
jokes.punchline: Will treating 'ritzy' as if it's an actual family name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Will's character. Good economy and shows his quick wit.

jokes.text: Okay. Ding, ding, ding! All sumo wrestlers to the showers!
jokes.setup: Phil and Fred getting into heated argument
jokes.punchline: Will comparing them to sumo wrestlers
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy comparing the two men's size and confrontation to sumo wrestling. Will as referee works.

jokes.text: I'll take that as a no.
jokes.setup: Phil claiming he's a judge when asked about honest work
jokes.punchline: Fred's dismissive response about judges not doing honest work
jokes.explanation: Good working-class vs. elite joke. Fred's dismissal of judges as not doing 'honest work' fits his character.

jokes.text: Crushing beer cans on your head...telling everybody why Sears is the place to buy tires.
jokes.setup: Phil threatening Fred wouldn't fit in at country club
jokes.punchline: Phil's stereotypical view of Fred's behavior
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's classist assumptions. The specific details about Sears tires add authenticity to the stereotype.

jokes.text: You wanna take this outside? We are outside. Fine. You wanna take it inside?
jokes.setup: Phil challenges Fred to take it outside
jokes.punchline: They're already outside, so Phil suggests going inside
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the classic tough-guy cliché with good escalation

jokes.text: Just my luck...Whitney Houston and I had just survived a nuclear holocaust. And now I was the last man on Earth.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey being woken up by doorbell
jokes.punchline: His elaborate fantasy scenario about surviving with Whitney Houston
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment - his elaborate romantic fantasy interrupted by mundane reality. Very character-specific and creative.

jokes.text: You call 5:30 early? Hell, the day is half gone.
jokes.setup: Phil complains about being disturbed early
jokes.punchline: Fred's working-class perspective on what constitutes early
jokes.explanation: Good class contrast humor showing the different lifestyles and work ethics

jokes.text: Hey, I met a nice gal through that ad. In some cultures...it's considered a real turn-on for a woman to have a sloped forehead.
jokes.setup: Ashley mocking Carlton's personal ad
jokes.punchline: Carlton's defensive explanation about his girlfriend's unconventional features
jokes.explanation: Great Carlton moment showing his desperate attempts to rationalize. The specific 'sloped forehead' detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I'm definitely the Marilyn Munster of this family.
jokes.setup: Ashley observing the family's crazy behavior
jokes.punchline: Her reference to being the normal one in a family of monsters
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly captures Ashley's role as the normal one. Very quotable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You're lucky she's dead. What? Uh, uh, I mean, my wife is still alive. What? She's gonna kill me when she finds out about this.
jokes.setup: Fred mentioning his dead wife would be upset
jokes.punchline: Phil's accidentally insensitive comment and awkward recovery
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works because of Phil's genuine horror at his mistake. The escalating awkwardness sells it.

jokes.text: Actually, I'd prefer a white wine spritzer.
jokes.setup: Fred offering Phil a beer as peace gesture
jokes.punchline: Phil's typically fancy drink preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil moment showing he can't help being fancy even when trying to bond. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Ain't no way I was getting married at a place called Temple Emanuel L. Lewis.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why they left the traditional wedding venue
jokes.punchline: His play on actor Emmanuel Lewis's name combined with temple
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference combining Emmanuel Lewis with Jewish temple names. Good wordplay and period-appropriate.

jokes.text: Who's the dick who's a sex machine to all the chicks? Shaft? You're damn right. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will getting excited about Shaft wedding package
jokes.punchline: Will doing the classic Shaft theme lyrics
jokes.explanation: Direct quote from Shaft theme. Funny in context but relies heavily on existing material rather than original comedy.

jokes.text: Well, okay, baby, but I don't wanna be given away by the pimp.
jokes.setup: Will wanting the Shaft wedding package
jokes.punchline: Lisa's concern about the inappropriate elements
jokes.explanation: Lisa being the voice of reason while still going along with Will's enthusiasm. Good balance of character traits.

jokes.text: although in November, she sold off a number of hairs... Shares.
jokes.setup: Carlton reading financial documents to IRS agent
jokes.punchline: His nervous mispronunciation and correction
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's nervousness under pressure. Simple verbal stumble but fits the character's anxiety.

jokes.text: That was a tape recorder?
jokes.setup: Carlton playing music during the 'harassment'
jokes.punchline: Revealing Carlton used a tape player, not recorder
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Carlton's plan backfiring. The technology confusion adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: You're dilly-dallying days are done.
jokes.setup: Carlton threatening Agent Dailey
jokes.punchline: His alliterative threat using her name
jokes.explanation: Carlton trying to sound tough with wordplay. The alliteration fits his character's tendency to over-complicate things.

jokes.text: Look, Lisa, baby, I'm telling you...I want to marry you, but definitely not like this. Right on, Will. Oh, by the way, dude, your Isaac Hayes impression stinks.
jokes.setup: Will stopping the elaborate Shaft-themed ceremony
jokes.punchline: Minister breaking character to critique Will's impression
jokes.explanation: Great meta-moment where the minister breaks character. Shows Will made the right choice while getting a dig in.

jokes.text: All right, that's enough! That's it. Look, Lisa, baby, I'm telling you...I want to marry you, but definitely not like this.
jokes.setup: The Shaft wedding ceremony becomes increasingly ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Will finally breaks character and stops the absurd ceremony
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the escalating absurdity - Will has limits even he won't cross

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, dude, your Isaac Hayes impression stinks.
jokes.setup: The Shaft officiant defends his impression
jokes.punchline: Will's honest critique of the performance
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the ceremony performance that feels natural from Will

jokes.text: Thank God I pressed the wrong button. The B-side is John Travolta's polka classics.
jokes.setup: Carlton accidentally playing Beach Boys instead of recording harassment
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous alternative of Travolta polka music
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd concept - John Travolta doing polka music. The specificity and randomness make it memorable.

jokes.text: So Daddy's coming home with a Turf Buddy.
jokes.setup: Phil explains he's getting a golf cart with the wedding deposit
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'Turf Buddy' for a golf cart
jokes.explanation: The product name is inherently funny and fits Phil's materialistic character

jokes.text: Only if he sits on it.
jokes.setup: Fred asking if Phil can pop a wheelie on the golf cart
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry comment about Phil's weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey closer - his dry wit about Phil's size delivered with maximum economy. Excellent button to the episode.

jokes.text: Nope.
jokes.setup: Will asks if anybody cares what they want
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's consistent brutal honesty returns
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to his earlier brutal honesty - Geoffrey remains consistently unhelpful and honest
